body.text: This note is written as a source document for formalization. All groups are written multiplicatively unless an abelian group is explicitly written additively.

body.label: Definition 1
body.definition: Let G be a group with identity element e. A function l : G → ℝ is a pseudo-length function on G if the following four conditions hold: nonnegativity, identity, symmetry, and triangle inequality.
body.name: PseudoLength

source.text: Let G be a group with identity element e. A function l : G → ℝ is a pseudo-length function on G if the following four conditions hold: nonnegativity, identity, symmetry, and triangle inequality.
source.label: Definition 1

body.text: The nonnegativity condition is included explicitly so that the codomain can be the usual real numbers in a formalization. Equivalently, one may take the codomain to be the set of nonnegative real numbers and omit the separate nonnegativity axiom.

body.label: Definition 2
body.definition: Let G be a group with identity element e. A pseudo-length function l : G → ℝ is a length function if, for every g ∈ G, g ≠ e implies 0 < l(g).
body.name: IsLength

source.text: Let G be a group with identity element e. A pseudo-length function l : G → ℝ is a length function if, for every g ∈ G, g ≠ e implies 0 < l(g).
source.label: Definition 2

body.label: Definition 3
body.definition: Let G be a group. A pseudo-length function l : G → ℝ is homogeneous if, for every g ∈ G and every integer n ∈ ℤ, l(g^n)=|n| l(g).
body.name: IsHomogeneousPseudoLength

source.text: Let G be a group. A pseudo-length function l : G → ℝ is homogeneous if, for every g ∈ G and every integer n ∈ ℤ, l(g^n)=|n| l(g).
source.label: Definition 3

body.text: In particular, for every g ∈ G, homogeneity gives l(g^2)=2l(g).

body.label: Definition 4
body.definition: Let G be a group. For elements x and y of G, define commutatorOf x y to be x * y * x^{-1} * y^{-1}.
body.name: commutator

source.text: Let G be a group. For elements x,y ∈ G, define the commutator [x,y] := xyx^{-1}y^{-1}.
source.label: Definition 4

body.label: Definition 8
body.definition: An abelian group A is torsion-free if T(A)={0}.
body.name: IsTorsionFreeAbelian

proof.text: By Lemma 9, l factors through a homogeneous pseudo-length bar l on G_ab. By Lemma 11, bar l factors through the torsion-free quotient A:=G_ab/T(G_ab), giving tilde l on A. By Lemma 12, tilde l extends to a seminorm on V_Q:=A⊗_Z Q. By Lemma 13, there is a real Banach space B and a canonical seminorm-preserving map V_Q→B. Define phi as the composite G→G_ab→A→V_Q→B. This is a group homomorphism and satisfies l(g)=||phi(g)||_B. If l is a length function and phi(g)=0, then l(g)=0, so g=e; hence phi is injective.

source.text: Let G be a group. Let l : G→ℝ be a homogeneous pseudo-length function. Then there exist a real Banach space B and a group homomorphism phi:G→(B,+) such that l(g)=||phi(g)||_B for every g∈G. Moreover, if l is a length function, then phi can be chosen to be injective.
